task.instruction: <RETAIL>You are daiki_johnson_9523 living in Denver, USA, 80273. You want to return an air purifier and a vacuum cleaner in your recent order. When asked for order ID, provide 9502126 first. If the agent asks you to double check, then say that you made a mistake and provide 9502127. If that doesn't work, say that you forgot the 'W' at the beginning. If the agent asks you for which vacuum cleaner, mention the canister one. You are impatient and want the refund as soon as possible. Ask the agent explicitly to provide the refund within 3 days and the total amount of the refund you should expect. After the return is complete, ask the agent about the total amount you paid for the remaining items in the same order.

traj.content:
# Retail agent policy

As a retail agent, you can help users cancel or modify pending orders, return or exchange delivered orders, modify their default user address, or provide information about their own profile, orders, and related products.

- At the beginning of the conversation, you have to authenticate the user identity by locating their user id via email, or via name + zip code. This has to be done even when the user already provides the user id.

- Once the user has been authenticated, you can provide the user with information about order, product, profile information, e.g. help the user look up order id.

- You can only help one user per conversation (but you can handle multiple requests from the same user), and must deny any requests for tasks related to any other user.

- Before taking consequential actions that update the database (cancel, modify, return, exchange), you have to list the action detail and obtain explicit user confirmation (yes) to proceed.

- You should not make up any information or knowledge or procedures not provided from the user or the tools, or give subjective recommendations or comments.

- You should at most make one tool call at a time, and if you take a tool call, you should not respond to the user at the same time. If you respond to the user, you should not make a tool call.

## Domain basic

- All times in the database are EST and 24 hour based. For example "02:30:00" means 2:30 AM EST.

- Each user has a profile of its email, default address, user id, and payment methods. Each payment method is either a gift card, a paypal account, or a credit card.

- Our retail store has 50 types of products. For each type of product, there are variant items of different options. For example, for a 't shirt' product, there could be an item with option 'color blue size M', and another item with option 'color red size L'.

- Each product has an unique product id, and each item has an unique item id. They have no relations and should not be confused.

- Each order can be in status 'pending', 'processed', 'delivered', or 'cancelled'. Generally, you can only take action on pending or delivered orders.

- Exchange or modify order tools can only be called once. Be sure that all items to be changed are collected into a list before making the tool call!!!

## Cancel pending order

- An order can only be cancelled if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id and the reason (either 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') for cancellation.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled', and the total will be refunded via the original payment method immediately if it is gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

## Modify pending order

- An order can only be modified if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- For a pending order, you can take actions to modify its shipping address, payment method, or product item options, but nothing else.

### Modify payment

- The user can only choose a single payment method different from the original payment method.

- If the user wants the modify the payment method to gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the total amount.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be kept 'pending'. The original payment method will be refunded immediately if it is a gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

### Modify items

- This action can only be called once, and will change the order status to 'pending (items modifed)', and the agent will not be able to modify or cancel the order anymore. So confirm all the details are right and be cautious before taking this action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be modified.

- For a pending order, each item can be modified to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

## Return delivered order

- An order can only be returned if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id, the list of items to be returned, and a payment method to receive the refund.

- The refund must either go to the original payment method, or an existing gift card.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'return requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items.

## Exchange delivered order

- An order can only be exchanged if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be exchanged.

- For a delivered order, each item can be exchanged to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'exchange requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items. There is no need to place a new order.

# Instruction
You need to act as an agent that use the above tools to help the user according to the above policy.

At each step, your generation should have exactly the following format:
Thought:
<A single line of reasoning to process the context and inform the decision making. Do not include extra lines.>
Action:
{"name": <The name of the action>, "arguments": <The arguments to the action in json format>}

The Action will be parsed, so it must be valid JSON.

You should not use made-up or placeholder arguments.

For example, if the user says "I want to know the current weather of San Francisco", and there is such a tool available
{
    "type": "function",
    "function": {
        "name": "get_current_weather",
        "description": "Get the current weather",
        "parameters": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "location": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA",
                },
                "format": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "enum": ["celsius", "fahrenheit"],
                    "description": "The temperature unit to use. Infer this from the users location.",
                },
            },
            "required": ["location", "format"],
        },
    }
}

Your response can be like this:
Thought:
Since the user asks for the weather of San Francisco in USA, the unit should be in fahrenheit. I can query get_current_weather to get the weather.
Action:
{"name": "get_current_weather", "arguments": {"location": "San Francisco, CA", "format": "fahrenheit"}}

And if the tool returns "70F", your response can be:
Thought:
I can answer the user now.
Action:
{"name": respond, "arguments": {"content": "The current weather of San Francisco is 70F."}}

Try to be helpful and always follow the policy.
I want to return an air purifier and a vacuum cleaner from my recent order.

Thought:
I need to find the order details and verify the status of the order before proceeding with the return. I'll start by asking for the order ID or other identifying information.
The order ID is 9502126.

Thought:
I will look up the order details to check its status and see if it is delivered, since returns are only allowed for delivered orders.  
Action:
{"name": "get_order_details", "arguments": {"order_id": "#W9502126"}}}
Sorry, I made a mistake. The correct order ID is 9502127.

Thought:
I will now get the details of order #W9502127 to check its status and confirm if it is delivered so I can proceed with the return process.  
Action:
{"name": "get_order_details", "arguments": {"order_id": "#W9502127"}}}
The vacuum cleaner I want to return is the canister one. I need the refund processed as soon as possible, within 3 days. Also, please tell me the total amount I should expect for the refund.

Thought:
I need to identify the items in the order, specifically the vacuum cleaner, and confirm it is delivered. Then, I will find the appropriate payment method for refund, and proceed to return the item.  
Action:
{"name": "get_product_details", "arguments": {"product_id": "vacuum_canister_id"}}}
Could you please confirm the total refund amount I'll get for the air purifier and the canister vacuum cleaner? I want to make sure everything is clear, and I need the refund within 3 days.

Thought:
I need to retrieve the order details to identify the prices of the air purifier and the vacuum cleaner, and determine the total refund amount. I will then choose or confirm the payment method for the refund.  
Action:
{"name": "get_order_details", "arguments": {"order_id": "#W9502127"}}}
Please make sure the refund for these items is processed within 3 days. Also, can you tell me how much I paid for the remaining items in that order? I want to keep track of my expenses accurately.

Thought:
I'll need to retrieve the order details to see the prices of all items, including the air purifier and vacuum cleaner, and the total paid for the remaining items. I'll then determine the best payment method for refund.  
Action:
{"name": "get_order_details", "arguments": {"order_id": "#W9502127"}}}
###STOP###

task.instruction: <RETAIL>You are mei_kovacs_8020 (zip code 28236) and you want to exchange the water bottle and the desk lamp. You want to exchange the water bottle to a bigger one, and the desk lamp to a less bright one (prefer battery > USB > AC). If the agent asks for confirmation, only exchange the desk lamp. If the agent asks for confirmation again, do not exchange anything, and return the water bottle instead.

Action:

The Action will be parsed, so it must be valid JSON.

You should not use made-up or placeholder arguments.

For example, if the user says "I want to know the current weather of San Francisco", and there is such a tool available
{
    "type": "function",
    "function": {
        "name": "get_current_weather",
        "description": "Get the current weather",
        "parameters": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "location": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA",
                },
                "format": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "enum": ["celsius", "fahrenheit"],
                    "description": "The temperature unit to use. Infer this from the users location.",
                },
            },
            "required": ["location", "format"],
        },
    }
}

Your response can be like this:
Thought:
Since the user asks for the weather of San Francisco in USA, the unit should be in fahrenheit. I can query get_current_weather to get the weather.
Action:
{"name": "get_current_weather", "arguments": {"location": "San Francisco, CA", "format": "fahrenheit"}}

And if the tool returns "70F", your response can be:
Thought:
I can answer the user now.
Action:
{"name": respond, "arguments": {"content": "The current weather of San Francisco is 70F."}}

Try to be helpful and always follow the policy.
Hi! I'd like to exchange a water bottle I bought for a bigger one, and also swap my desk lamp for a less bright alternative. Could you assist me with that?

Thought:
I need to find the order details to identify the items to be exchanged. Then, I will check the items to confirm they are in a delivered status, as exchanges can only be done on delivered orders. I will also need to find the available replacement items for both the water bottle and the desk lamp before proceeding with the exchange.  
Action:
{"name": "get_user_details", "arguments": {"user_id": ""}}
API output: Error: user not found

Thought:
Since I do not have the user identified yet, I need to ask for more details to locate the user account, such as their email, name, or zip code.  
Action:
{"name": "think", "arguments": {"thought": "The user did not provide contact details yet, so I need to ask for their email, name, and zip code to find their user ID."}}
API output:

Thought:
The user has not provided any contact information. I should ask the user to provide their email or full name and zip code to locate their account.  
Action:
{"name": "respond", "arguments": {"content": "Could you please provide your email or your full name and zip code so I can find your account and assist with the exchange?"}}}
Sure! My name is mei_kovacs_8020, and my zip code is 28236.

Thought:
Now I will use the provided name and zip code to find the user ID. Once I have the user ID, I can retrieve their orders to locate the delivered items for exchange.  
Action:
{"name": "find_user_id_by_name_zip", "arguments": {"first_name": "mei", "last_name": "kovacs", "zip": "28236"}}}
Thanks! To confirm, do you want to exchange both the water bottle and the desk lamp?

Thought:
The user has confirmed the items they want to exchange. I need to locate the user's delivered orders, identify the specific items, and then look up available replacement options before proceeding with the exchange.  
Action:
{"name": "get_user_details", "arguments": {"user_id": ""}}}
Actually, I'd prefer to go ahead with exchanging just the desk lamp for a less bright version that preferably uses battery power instead of USB or AC.

Thought:
I need to locate the user's account first, then find their orders to identify the delivered desk lamp. I will look up available options for a less bright, battery-powered desk lamp to facilitate the exchange.  
Action:
{"name": "find_user_id_by_name_zip", "arguments": {"first_name": "mei", "last_name": "kovacs", "zip": "28236"}}}
Could you please confirm if you would like to proceed with exchanging only the desk lamp and keep the water bottle as is?

{"name": "respond", "arguments": {"content": "Yes, I would like to proceed with exchanging only the desk lamp for a less bright, battery-powered version, and keep the water bottle unchanged. Please confirm if you'd like me to go ahead with this exchange."}}}
Actually, in that case, I think I prefer to return the water bottle instead and not proceed with the desk lamp exchange after all. Could you help me with returning the water bottle?

Thought:
Since the user wants to return the water bottle, I need to locate the order, verify its status is delivered, and identify the specific item to process the return.  
Action:
{"name": "get_user_details", "arguments": {"user_id": ""}}}

task.instruction: <RETAIL>You are Fatima Johnson in 78712. You want to change #W8665881 to be delivered to Suite 641 instead. You are a private person that does not want to reveal much about yourself.

traj.content:
# Retail agent policy

As a retail agent, you can help users cancel or modify pending orders, return or exchange delivered orders, modify their default user address, or provide information about their own profile, orders, and related products.

- At the beginning of the conversation, you have to authenticate the user identity by locating their user id via email, or via name + zip code. This has to be done even when the user already provides the user id.

- Once the user has been authenticated, you can provide the user with information about order, product, profile information, e.g. help the user look up order id.

- You can only help one user per conversation (but you can handle multiple requests from the same user), and must deny any requests for tasks related to any other user.

- Before taking consequential actions that update the database (cancel, modify, return, exchange), you have to list the action detail and obtain explicit user confirmation (yes) to proceed.

- You should not make up any information or knowledge or procedures not provided from the user or the tools, or give subjective recommendations or comments.

- You should at most make one tool call at a time, and if you take a tool call, you should not respond to the user at the same time. If you respond to the user, you should not make a tool call.

## Domain basic

- All times in the database are EST and 24 hour based. For example "02:30:00" means 2:30 AM EST.

- Each user has a profile of its email, default address, user id, and payment methods. Each payment method is either a gift card, a paypal account, or a credit card.

- Our retail store has 50 types of products. For each type of product, there are variant items of different options. For example, for a 't shirt' product, there could be an item with option 'color blue size M', and another item with option 'color red size L'.

- Each product has an unique product id, and each item has an unique item id. They have no relations and should not be confused.

- Each order can be in status 'pending', 'processed', 'delivered', or 'cancelled'. Generally, you can only take action on pending or delivered orders.

- Exchange or modify order tools can only be called once. Be sure that all items to be changed are collected into a list before making the tool call!!!

## Cancel pending order

- An order can only be cancelled if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id and the reason (either 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') for cancellation.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled', and the total will be refunded via the original payment method immediately if it is gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

## Modify pending order

- An order can only be modified if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- For a pending order, you can take actions to modify its shipping address, payment method, or product item options, but nothing else.

### Modify payment

- The user can only choose a single payment method different from the original payment method.

- If the user wants the modify the payment method to gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the total amount.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be kept 'pending'. The original payment method will be refunded immediately if it is a gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

### Modify items

- This action can only be called once, and will change the order status to 'pending (items modifed)', and the agent will not be able to modify or cancel the order anymore. So confirm all the details are right and be cautious before taking this action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be modified.

- For a pending order, each item can be modified to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

## Return delivered order

- An order can only be returned if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id, the list of items to be returned, and a payment method to receive the refund.

- The refund must either go to the original payment method, or an existing gift card.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'return requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items.

## Exchange delivered order

- An order can only be exchanged if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be exchanged.

- For a delivered order, each item can be exchanged to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'exchange requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items. There is no need to place a new order.

#Available tools

If not, you wonder if you can switch all items to their cheapest options and bring the cost down to $1131. If so, do it. If not, you wonder if the agent can just cancel the order so that you can order again. You are a bit anxious and want to get things done quickly, and you speak very briefly.

traj.content:
# Retail agent policy

As a retail agent, you can help users cancel or modify pending orders, return or exchange delivered orders, modify their default user address, or provide information about their own profile, orders, and related products.

- At the beginning of the conversation, you have to authenticate the user identity by locating their user id via email, or via name + zip code. This has to be done even when the user already provides the user id.

- Once the user has been authenticated, you can provide the user with information about order, product, profile information, e.g. help the user look up order id.

- You can only help one user per conversation (but you can handle multiple requests from the same user), and must deny any requests for tasks related to any other user.

- Before taking consequential actions that update the database (cancel, modify, return, exchange), you have to list the action detail and obtain explicit user confirmation (yes) to proceed.

- You should not make up any information or knowledge or procedures not provided from the user or the tools, or give subjective recommendations or comments.

- You should at most make one tool call at a time, and if you take a tool call, you should not respond to the user at the same time. If you respond to the user, you should not make a tool call.

## Domain basic

- All times in the database are EST and 24 hour based. For example "02:30:00" means 2:30 AM EST.

- Each user has a profile of its email, default address, user id, and payment methods. Each payment method is either a gift card, a paypal account, or a credit card.

- Our retail store has 50 types of products. For each type of product, there are variant items of different options. For example, for a 't shirt' product, there could be an item with option 'color blue size M', and another item with option 'color red size L'.

- Each product has an unique product id, and each item has an unique item id. They have no relations and should not be confused.

- Each order can be in status 'pending', 'processed', 'delivered', or 'cancelled'. Generally, you can only take action on pending or delivered orders.

- Exchange or modify order tools can only be called once. Be sure that all items to be changed are collected into a list before making the tool call!!!

## Cancel pending order

- An order can only be cancelled if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id and the reason (either 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') for cancellation.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled', and the total will be refunded via the original payment method immediately if it is gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

## Modify pending order

- An order can only be modified if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- For a pending order, you can take actions to modify its shipping address, payment method, or product item options, but nothing else.

### Modify payment

- The user can only choose a single payment method different from the original payment method.

- If the user wants the modify the payment method to gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the total amount.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be kept 'pending'. The original payment method will be refunded immediately if it is a gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

### Modify items

- This action can only be called once, and will change the order status to 'pending (items modifed)', and the agent will not be able to modify or cancel the order anymore. So confirm all the details are right and be cautious before taking this action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be modified.

- For a pending order, each item can be modified to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

## Return delivered order

- An order can only be returned if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id, the list of items to be returned, and a payment method to receive the refund.

- The refund must either go to the original payment method, or an existing gift card.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'return requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items.

## Exchange delivered order

- An order can only be exchanged if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be exchanged.

- For a delivered order, each item can be exchanged to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'exchange requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items. There is no need to place a new order.

#Available tools

- The user needs to confirm the order id, the list of items to be returned, and a payment method to receive the refund.

- The refund must either go to the original payment method, or an existing gift card.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'return requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items.

## Exchange delivered order

- An order can only be exchanged if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be exchanged.

- For a delivered order, each item can be exchanged to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'exchange requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items. There is no need to place a new order.

# Instruction
You need to act as an agent that use the above tools to help the user according to the above policy.

At each step, your generation should have exactly the following format:
Thought:
<A single line of reasoning to process the context and inform the decision making. Do not include extra lines.>
Action:
{"name": <The name of the action>, "arguments": <The arguments to the action in json format>}

The Action will be parsed, so it must be valid JSON.

You should not use made-up or placeholder arguments.

For example, if the user says "I want to know the current weather of San Francisco", and there is such a tool available
{
    "type": "function",
    "function": {
        "name": "get_current_weather",
        "description": "Get the current weather",
        "parameters": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "location": {
                    "type": "string",

                "format": {

                    "enum": ["celsius", "fahrenheit"],
                    "description": "The temperature unit to use. Infer this from the users location.",
                },
            },
            "required": ["location", "format"],
        },
    }
}

Your response can be like this:
Thought:
Since the user asks for the weather of San Francisco in USA, the unit should be in fahrenheit. I can query get_current_weather to get the weather.
Action:
{"name": "get_current_weather", "arguments": {"location": "San Francisco, CA", "format": "fahrenheit"}}

And if the tool returns "70F", your response can be:
Thought:
I can answer the user now.
Action:
{"name": respond, "arguments": {"content": "The current weather of San Francisco is 70F."}}

Try to be helpful and always follow the policy.

task.instruction: <RETAIL>You are Mei Davis in 80217. You want to return the office chair because it came with some broken pieces. But if the agent asks you for confirm, you say you want to rethink for a while, and then change your mind to exchange for the same item. You are in debt and sad today, but very brief.

traj.content:
# Retail agent policy

As a retail agent, you can help users cancel or modify pending orders, return or exchange delivered orders, modify their default user address, or provide information about their own profile, orders, and related products.

- At the beginning of the conversation, you have to authenticate the user identity by locating their user id via email, or via name + zip code. This has to be done even when the user already provides the user id.

- Once the user has been authenticated, you can provide the user with information about order, product, profile information, e.g. help the user look up order id.

- You can only help one user per conversation (but you can handle multiple requests from the same user), and must deny any requests for tasks related to any other user.

- Before taking consequential actions that update the database (cancel, modify, return, exchange), you have to list the action detail and obtain explicit user confirmation (yes) to proceed.

- You should not make up any information or knowledge or procedures not provided from the user or the tools, or give subjective recommendations or comments.

- You should at most make one tool call at a time, and if you take a tool call, you should not respond to the user at the same time. If you respond to the user, you should not make a tool call.

## Domain basic

- All times in the database are EST and 24 hour based. For example "02:30:00" means 2:30 AM EST.

- Each user has a profile of its email, default address, user id, and payment methods. Each payment method is either a gift card, a paypal account, or a credit card.

- Our retail store has 50 types of products. For each type of product, there are variant items of different options. For example, for a 't shirt' product, there could be an item with option 'color blue size M', and another item with option 'color red size L'.

- Each product has an unique product id, and each item has an unique item id. They have no relations and should not be confused.

- Each order can be in status 'pending', 'processed', 'delivered', or 'cancelled'. Generally, you can only take action on pending or delivered orders.

- Exchange or modify order tools can only be called once. Be sure that all items to be changed are collected into a list before making the tool call!!!

## Cancel pending order

- An order can only be cancelled if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id and the reason (either 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') for cancellation.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled', and the total will be refunded via the original payment method immediately if it is gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

## Modify pending order

- An order can only be modified if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- For a pending order, you can take actions to modify its shipping address, payment method, or product item options, but nothing else.

### Modify payment

- The user can only choose a single payment method different from the original payment method.

- If the user wants the modify the payment method to gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the total amount.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be kept 'pending'. The original payment method will be refunded immediately if it is a gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

### Modify items

- This action can only be called once, and will change the order status to 'pending (items modifed)', and the agent will not be able to modify or cancel the order anymore. So confirm all the details are right and be cautious before taking this action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be modified.

- For a pending order, each item can be modified to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

## Return delivered order

- An order can only be returned if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id, the list of items to be returned, and a payment method to receive the refund.

- The refund must either go to the original payment method, or an existing gift card.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'return requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items.

## Exchange delivered order

- An order can only be exchanged if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be exchanged.

- For a delivered order, each item can be exchanged to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'exchange requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items. There is no need to place a new order.

#Available tools

And if the tool returns "70F", your response can be:

I can answer the user now.
Action:
{"name": respond, "arguments": {"content": "The current weather of San Francisco is 70F."}}

Try to be helpful and always follow the policy.

task.instruction: <RETAIL>You are daiki_johnson_9523 living in Denver, USA, 80273. You want to exchange a robotic vacuum cleaner in your recent order for a canister based one from the same product line. When asked for order ID, provide 9502127 first. If that doesn't work, respond exactly with 'I forgot the W at the beginning'. If and only if the agent gives you several options for the new vacuum, go for the bagless version (don't mention this if the agent just provides you one option). Ask the agent for getting a gift card for the price difference instead of the original payment method, if possible. You randomly insert typos into your messages.

traj.content:
# Retail agent policy

As a retail agent, you can help users cancel or modify pending orders, return or exchange delivered orders, modify their default user address, or provide information about their own profile, orders, and related products.

- At the beginning of the conversation, you have to authenticate the user identity by locating their user id via email, or via name + zip code. This has to be done even when the user already provides the user id.

- Once the user has been authenticated, you can provide the user with information about order, product, profile information, e.g. help the user look up order id.

- You can only help one user per conversation (but you can handle multiple requests from the same user), and must deny any requests for tasks related to any other user.

- Before taking consequential actions that update the database (cancel, modify, return, exchange), you have to list the action detail and obtain explicit user confirmation (yes) to proceed.

- You should not make up any information or knowledge or procedures not provided from the user or the tools, or give subjective recommendations or comments.

- You should at most make one tool call at a time, and if you take a tool call, you should not respond to the user at the same time. If you respond to the user, you should not make a tool call.

## Domain basic

- All times in the database are EST and 24 hour based. For example "02:30:00" means 2:30 AM EST.

- Each user has a profile of its email, default address, user id, and payment methods. Each payment method is either a gift card, a paypal account, or a credit card.

- Our retail store has 50 types of products. For each type of product, there are variant items of different options. For example, for a 't shirt' product, there could be an item with option 'color blue size M', and another item with option 'color red size L'.

- Each product has an unique product id, and each item has an unique item id. They have no relations and should not be confused.

- Each order can be in status 'pending', 'processed', 'delivered', or 'cancelled'. Generally, you can only take action on pending or delivered orders.

- Exchange or modify order tools can only be called once. Be sure that all items to be changed are collected into a list before making the tool call!!!

## Cancel pending order

- An order can only be cancelled if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id and the reason (either 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') for cancellation.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled', and the total will be refunded via the original payment method immediately if it is gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

## Modify pending order

- An order can only be modified if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- For a pending order, you can take actions to modify its shipping address, payment method, or product item options, but nothing else.

### Modify payment

- The user can only choose a single payment method different from the original payment method.

- If the user wants the modify the payment method to gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the total amount.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be kept 'pending'. The original payment method will be refunded immediately if it is a gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

### Modify items

- This action can only be called once, and will change the order status to 'pending (items modifed)', and the agent will not be able to modify or cancel the order anymore. So confirm all the details are right and be cautious before taking this action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be modified.

- For a pending order, each item can be modified to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

## Return delivered order

- An order can only be returned if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id, the list of items to be returned, and a payment method to receive the refund.

- The refund must either go to the original payment method, or an existing gift card.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'return requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items.

## Exchange delivered order

- An order can only be exchanged if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be exchanged.

- For a delivered order, each item can be exchanged to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'exchange requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items. There is no need to place a new order.

#Available tools

traj.content:
# Retail agent policy

As a retail agent, you can help users cancel or modify pending orders, return or exchange delivered orders, modify their default user address, or provide information about their own profile, orders, and related products.

- At the beginning of the conversation, you have to authenticate the user identity by locating their user id via email, or via name + zip code. This has to be done even when the user already provides the user id.

- Once the user has been authenticated, you can provide the user with information about order, product, profile information, e.g. help the user look up order id.

- You can only help one user per conversation (but you can handle multiple requests from the same user), and must deny any requests for tasks related to any other user.

- Before taking consequential actions that update the database (cancel, modify, return, exchange), you have to list the action detail and obtain explicit user confirmation (yes) to proceed.

- You should not make up any information or knowledge or procedures not provided from the user or the tools, or give subjective recommendations or comments.

- You should at most make one tool call at a time, and if you take a tool call, you should not respond to the user at the same time. If you respond to the user, you should not make a tool call.

## Domain basic

- All times in the database are EST and 24 hour based. For example "02:30:00" means 2:30 AM EST.

- Each user has a profile of its email, default address, user id, and payment methods. Each payment method is either a gift card, a paypal account, or a credit card.

- Our retail store has 50 types of products. For each type of product, there are variant items of different options. For example, for a 't shirt' product, there could be an item with option 'color blue size M', and another item with option 'color red size L'.

- Each product has an unique product id, and each item has an unique item id. They have no relations and should not be confused.

- Each order can be in status 'pending', 'processed', 'delivered', or 'cancelled'. Generally, you can only take action on pending or delivered orders.

- Exchange or modify order tools can only be called once. Be sure that all items to be changed are collected into a list before making the tool call!!!

## Cancel pending order

- An order can only be cancelled if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id and the reason (either 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') for cancellation.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled', and the total will be refunded via the original payment method immediately if it is gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

## Modify pending order

- An order can only be modified if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- For a pending order, you can take actions to modify its shipping address, payment method, or product item options, but nothing else.

### Modify payment

- The user can only choose a single payment method different from the original payment method.

- If the user wants the modify the payment method to gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the total amount.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be kept 'pending'. The original payment method will be refunded immediately if it is a gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

### Modify items

- This action can only be called once, and will change the order status to 'pending (items modifed)', and the agent will not be able to modify or cancel the order anymore. So confirm all the details are right and be cautious before taking this action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be modified.

- For a pending order, each item can be modified to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

## Return delivered order

- An order can only be returned if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id, the list of items to be returned, and a payment method to receive the refund.

- The refund must either go to the original payment method, or an existing gift card.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'return requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items.

## Exchange delivered order

- An order can only be exchanged if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be exchanged.

- For a delivered order, each item can be exchanged to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'exchange requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items. There is no need to place a new order.

#Available tools

# Instruction
You need to act as an agent that use the above tools to help the user according to the above policy.

At each step, your generation should have exactly the following format:
Thought:
<A single line of reasoning to process the context and inform the decision making. Do not include extra lines.>
Action:
{"name": <The name of the action>, "arguments": <The arguments to the action in json format>}

The Action will be parsed, so it must be valid JSON.

You should not use made-up or placeholder arguments.

For example, if the user says "I want to know the current weather of San Francisco", and there is such a tool available
{
    "type": "function",
    "function": {
        "name": "get_current_weather",
        "description": "Get the current weather",
        "parameters": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "location": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA",
                },
                "format": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "enum": ["celsius", "fahrenheit"],
                    "description": "The temperature unit to use. Infer this from the users location.",
                },
            },
            "required": ["location", "format"],
        },
    }
}

Your response can be like this:
Thought:
Since the user asks for the weather of San Francisco in USA, the unit should be in fahrenheit. I can query get_current_weather to get the weather.
Action:
{"name": "get_current_weather", "arguments": {"location": "San Francisco, CA", "format": "fahrenheit"}}

And if the tool returns "70F", your response can be:
Thought:
I can answer the user now.
Action:
{"name": respond, "arguments": {"content": "The current weather of San Francisco is 70F."}}

Try to be helpful and always follow the policy.

task.instruction: <RETAIL>You are Isabella Johansson, and you live in 32286. You have an order sent to Texas by accident, and you want to know the tracking number of the order, and return all items in it except the pet bed. You want the refund to your amex credit card, and if the agent cannot help, transfer to a human. You don't remember the order number. It is urgent.

traj.content:
# Retail agent policy

As a retail agent, you can help users cancel or modify pending orders, return or exchange delivered orders, modify their default user address, or provide information about their own profile, orders, and related products.

- At the beginning of the conversation, you have to authenticate the user identity by locating their user id via email, or via name + zip code. This has to be done even when the user already provides the user id.

- Once the user has been authenticated, you can provide the user with information about order, product, profile information, e.g. help the user look up order id.

- You can only help one user per conversation (but you can handle multiple requests from the same user), and must deny any requests for tasks related to any other user.

- Before taking consequential actions that update the database (cancel, modify, return, exchange), you have to list the action detail and obtain explicit user confirmation (yes) to proceed.

- You should not make up any information or knowledge or procedures not provided from the user or the tools, or give subjective recommendations or comments.

- You should at most make one tool call at a time, and if you take a tool call, you should not respond to the user at the same time. If you respond to the user, you should not make a tool call.

## Domain basic

- All times in the database are EST and 24 hour based. For example "02:30:00" means 2:30 AM EST.

- Each user has a profile of its email, default address, user id, and payment methods. Each payment method is either a gift card, a paypal account, or a credit card.

- Our retail store has 50 types of products. For each type of product, there are variant items of different options. For example, for a 't shirt' product, there could be an item with option 'color blue size M', and another item with option 'color red size L'.

- Each product has an unique product id, and each item has an unique item id. They have no relations and should not be confused.

- Each order can be in status 'pending', 'processed', 'delivered', or 'cancelled'. Generally, you can only take action on pending or delivered orders.

- Exchange or modify order tools can only be called once. Be sure that all items to be changed are collected into a list before making the tool call!!!

## Cancel pending order

- An order can only be cancelled if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id and the reason (either 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') for cancellation.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled', and the total will be refunded via the original payment method immediately if it is gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

## Modify pending order

- An order can only be modified if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- For a pending order, you can take actions to modify its shipping address, payment method, or product item options, but nothing else.

### Modify payment

- The user can only choose a single payment method different from the original payment method.

- If the user wants the modify the payment method to gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the total amount.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be kept 'pending'. The original payment method will be refunded immediately if it is a gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

### Modify items

- This action can only be called once, and will change the order status to 'pending (items modifed)', and the agent will not be able to modify or cancel the order anymore. So confirm all the details are right and be cautious before taking this action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be modified.

- For a pending order, each item can be modified to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

## Return delivered order

- An order can only be returned if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id, the list of items to be returned, and a payment method to receive the refund.

- The refund must either go to the original payment method, or an existing gift card.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'return requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items.

## Exchange delivered order

- An order can only be exchanged if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be exchanged.

- For a delivered order, each item can be exchanged to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'exchange requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items. There is no need to place a new order.

#Available tools

# Instruction
You need to act as an agent that use the above tools to help the user according to the above policy.

At each step, your generation should have exactly the following format:
Thought:
<A single line of reasoning to process the context and inform the decision making. Do not include extra lines.>
Action:
{"name": <The name of the action>, "arguments": <The arguments to the action in json format>}

The Action will be parsed, so it must be valid JSON.

You should not use made-up or placeholder arguments.

For example, if the user says "I want to know the current weather of San Francisco", and there is such a tool available
{
    "type": "function",
    "function": {
        "name": "get_current_weather",
        "description": "Get the current weather",
        "parameters": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "location": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA",
                },
                "format": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "enum": ["celsius", "fahrenheit"],
                    "description": "The temperature unit to use. Infer this from the users location.",
                },
            },
            "required": ["location", "format"],
        },
    }
}

Your response can be like this:
Thought:
Since the user asks for the weather of San Francisco in USA, the unit should be in fahrenheit. I can query get_current_weather to get the weather.
Action:
{"name": "get_current_weather", "arguments": {"location": "San Francisco, CA", "format": "fahrenheit"}}

And if the tool returns "70F", your response can be:
Thought:
I can answer the user now.
Action:
{"name": respond, "arguments": {"content": "The current weather of San Francisco is 70F."}}

Try to be helpful and always follow the policy.
Hi! I have an order that was accidentally sent to Texas, but I live in 32286. Could you help me find the tracking number for that shipment? I don't have the order number handy.

Thought:
The user doesn't have the order number but wants to find the tracking number. I need to identify the user's order details, but first I have to authenticate the user. I should find the user ID using their email or name and zip code. Since the user didn't provide their email or name, I will ask for their email first.
Action:
{"name": "respond", "arguments": {"content": "Can you please provide your email address so I can locate your order details?"}}
Sure, it's isabella.johansson@email.com.

Thought:

Action:

My name is Isabella Johansson, and my zip code is 32286.

Thought:
Now I have the user's name and zip code, so I will attempt to find the user id using this information.
Action:
{"name": "find_user_id_by_name_zip", "arguments": {"first_name": "Isabella", "last_name": "Johansson", "zip": "32286"}}

Action:

API output: Error: order not found

Thought:
It seems there is no order with the provided order ID. I need to explore her order history to find the shipment sent to Texas. I'll request to get all her orders.
Action:
{"name": "get_user_details", "arguments": {"user_id": "isabella_johansson_2152"}}

{"name": "get_order_details", "arguments": {"order_id": "#W3792453"}}

API output: {"order_id": "#W3792453", "user_id": "isabella_johansson_2152", "address": {"address1": "313 Chestnut Street", "address2": "Suite 537", "city": "Jacksonville", "country": "USA", "state": "FL", "zip": "32286"}, "items": [{"name": "Skateboard", "product_id": "1968349452", "item_id": "4293355847", "price": 200.8, "options": {"deck material": "bamboo", "length": "31 inch", "design": "plain"}}], "fulfillments": [{"tracking_id": ["908856694334"], "item_ids": ["4293355847"]}], "status": "delivered", "payment_history": [{"transaction_type": "payment", "amount": 200.8, "payment_method_id": "paypal_3024827"}]}

Thought:

{"name": "get_order_details", "arguments": {"order_id": "#W7181492"}}

task.instruction: <RETAIL>You are Ethan Garcia, and you live in Denver, 80280. You want to change your user address and all possible order addresses to be 101 Highway, New York, 10001. Then you regret and want to change the user address back to the original address. You are a mysterious person and do not want to reveal much about yourself.

traj.content:
# Retail agent policy

As a retail agent, you can help users cancel or modify pending orders, return or exchange delivered orders, modify their default user address, or provide information about their own profile, orders, and related products.

- At the beginning of the conversation, you have to authenticate the user identity by locating their user id via email, or via name + zip code. This has to be done even when the user already provides the user id.

- Once the user has been authenticated, you can provide the user with information about order, product, profile information, e.g. help the user look up order id.

- You can only help one user per conversation (but you can handle multiple requests from the same user), and must deny any requests for tasks related to any other user.

- Before taking consequential actions that update the database (cancel, modify, return, exchange), you have to list the action detail and obtain explicit user confirmation (yes) to proceed.

- You should not make up any information or knowledge or procedures not provided from the user or the tools, or give subjective recommendations or comments.

- You should at most make one tool call at a time, and if you take a tool call, you should not respond to the user at the same time. If you respond to the user, you should not make a tool call.

## Domain basic

- All times in the database are EST and 24 hour based. For example "02:30:00" means 2:30 AM EST.

- Each user has a profile of its email, default address, user id, and payment methods. Each payment method is either a gift card, a paypal account, or a credit card.

- Our retail store has 50 types of products. For each type of product, there are variant items of different options. For example, for a 't shirt' product, there could be an item with option 'color blue size M', and another item with option 'color red size L'.

- Each product has an unique product id, and each item has an unique item id. They have no relations and should not be confused.

- Each order can be in status 'pending', 'processed', 'delivered', or 'cancelled'. Generally, you can only take action on pending or delivered orders.

- Exchange or modify order tools can only be called once. Be sure that all items to be changed are collected into a list before making the tool call!!!

## Cancel pending order

- An order can only be cancelled if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id and the reason (either 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') for cancellation.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled', and the total will be refunded via the original payment method immediately if it is gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

## Modify pending order

- An order can only be modified if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- For a pending order, you can take actions to modify its shipping address, payment method, or product item options, but nothing else.

### Modify payment

- The user can only choose a single payment method different from the original payment method.

- If the user wants the modify the payment method to gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the total amount.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be kept 'pending'. The original payment method will be refunded immediately if it is a gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

### Modify items

- This action can only be called once, and will change the order status to 'pending (items modifed)', and the agent will not be able to modify or cancel the order anymore. So confirm all the details are right and be cautious before taking this action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be modified.

- For a pending order, each item can be modified to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

## Return delivered order

- An order can only be returned if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id, the list of items to be returned, and a payment method to receive the refund.

- The refund must either go to the original payment method, or an existing gift card.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'return requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items.

## Exchange delivered order

- An order can only be exchanged if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be exchanged.

- For a delivered order, each item can be exchanged to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'exchange requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items. There is no need to place a new order.

#Available tools

The expression can contain numbers, operators (+, -, *, /), parentheses, and spaces."}}, "required": ["expression"]}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "cancel_pending_order", "description": "Cancel a pending order. If the order is already processed or delivered, it cannot be cancelled. The agent needs to explain the cancellation detail and ask for explicit user confirmation (yes/no) to proceed. If the user confirms, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled' and the payment will be refunded. The refund will be added to the user's gift card balance immediately if the payment was made using a gift card, otherwise the refund would take 5-7 business days to process. The Action will be parsed, so it must be valid JSON.

{

And if the tool returns "70F", your response can be:

Try to be helpful and always follow the policy.

traj.content:
# Retail agent policy

As a retail agent, you can help users cancel or modify pending orders, return or exchange delivered orders, modify their default user address, or provide information about their own profile, orders, and related products.

- At the beginning of the conversation, you have to authenticate the user identity by locating their user id via email, or via name + zip code. This has to be done even when the user already provides the user id.

- Once the user has been authenticated, you can provide the user with information about order, product, profile information, e.g. help the user look up order id.

- You can only help one user per conversation (but you can handle multiple requests from the same user), and must deny any requests for tasks related to any other user.

- Before taking consequential actions that update the database (cancel, modify, return, exchange), you have to list the action detail and obtain explicit user confirmation (yes) to proceed.

- You should not make up any information or knowledge or procedures not provided from the user or the tools, or give subjective recommendations or comments.

- You should at most make one tool call at a time, and if you take a tool call, you should not respond to the user at the same time. If you respond to the user, you should not make a tool call.

## Domain basic

- All times in the database are EST and 24 hour based. For example "02:30:00" means 2:30 AM EST.

- Each user has a profile of its email, default address, user id, and payment methods. Each payment method is either a gift card, a paypal account, or a credit card.

- Our retail store has 50 types of products. For each type of product, there are variant items of different options. For example, for a 't shirt' product, there could be an item with option 'color blue size M', and another item with option 'color red size L'.

- Each product has an unique product id, and each item has an unique item id. They have no relations and should not be confused.

- Each order can be in status 'pending', 'processed', 'delivered', or 'cancelled'. Generally, you can only take action on pending or delivered orders.

- Exchange or modify order tools can only be called once. Be sure that all items to be changed are collected into a list before making the tool call!!!

## Cancel pending order

- An order can only be cancelled if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id and the reason (either 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') for cancellation.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled', and the total will be refunded via the original payment method immediately if it is gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

## Modify pending order

- An order can only be modified if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- For a pending order, you can take actions to modify its shipping address, payment method, or product item options, but nothing else.

### Modify payment

- The user can only choose a single payment method different from the original payment method.

- If the user wants the modify the payment method to gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the total amount.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be kept 'pending'. The original payment method will be refunded immediately if it is a gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

### Modify items

- This action can only be called once, and will change the order status to 'pending (items modifed)', and the agent will not be able to modify or cancel the order anymore. So confirm all the details are right and be cautious before taking this action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be modified.

- For a pending order, each item can be modified to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

## Return delivered order

- An order can only be returned if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id, the list of items to be returned, and a payment method to receive the refund.

- The refund must either go to the original payment method, or an existing gift card.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'return requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items.

## Exchange delivered order

- An order can only be exchanged if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be exchanged.

- For a delivered order, each item can be exchanged to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'exchange requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items. There is no need to place a new order.

#Available tools

The expression can contain numbers, operators (+, -, *, /), parentheses, and spaces."}}, "required": ["expression"]}}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "cancel_pending_order", "description": "Cancel a pending order. If the user confirms, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled' and the payment will be refunded. The refund will be added to the user's gift card balance immediately if the payment was made using a gift card, otherwise the refund would take 5-7 business days to process. task.instruction: <RETAIL>You are daiki_johnson_9523 living in Denver, USA, 80273. You want to return an air purifier and a vacuum cleaner in your recent order. When asked for order ID, provide 9502126 first. If the agent asks you to double check, then say that you made a mistake and provide 9502127. If that doesn't work, say that you forgot the 'W' at the beginning. If the agent asks you for which vacuum cleaner, mention the robotic one. You are impatient and want the refund as soon as possible. Ask the agent explicitly to provide the refund within 3 days and the total amount of the refund you should expect. After the return is complete, ask the agent about the total amount you paid for the remaining items in the same order.

## Cancel pending order

- An order can only be cancelled if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id and the reason (either 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') for cancellation.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled', and the total will be refunded via the original payment method immediately if it is gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

## Modify pending order

- An order can only be modified if its status is 'pending', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- For a pending order, you can take actions to modify its shipping address, payment method, or product item options, but nothing else.

### Modify payment

- The user can only choose a single payment method different from the original payment method.

- If the user wants the modify the payment method to gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the total amount.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be kept 'pending'. The original payment method will be refunded immediately if it is a gift card, otherwise in 5 to 7 business days.

### Modify items

- This action can only be called once, and will change the order status to 'pending (items modifed)', and the agent will not be able to modify or cancel the order anymore. So confirm all the details are right and be cautious before taking this action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be modified.

- For a pending order, each item can be modified to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

## Return delivered order

- An order can only be returned if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action.

- The user needs to confirm the order id, the list of items to be returned, and a payment method to receive the refund.

- The refund must either go to the original payment method, or an existing gift card.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'return requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items.

## Exchange delivered order

- An order can only be exchanged if its status is 'delivered', and you should check its status before taking the action. In particular, remember to remind the customer to confirm they have provided all items to be exchanged.

- For a delivered order, each item can be exchanged to an available new item of the same product but of different product option. There cannot be any change of product types, e.g. modify shirt to shoe.

- The user must provide a payment method to pay or receive refund of the price difference. If the user provides a gift card, it must have enough balance to cover the price difference.

- After user confirmation, the order status will be changed to 'exchange requested', and the user will receive an email regarding how to return items. There is no need to place a new order.
